meta.title: DOM & Events Practice
meta.description: Practice set focusing on DOM manipulation, event handling, and localStorage.

options.label: A
options.text: Creates a `<div>` element and immediately adds it to the page

options.label: B
options.text: Creates a `<div>` element in memory (not yet in the DOM)

options.label: C
options.text: Selects the first `<div>` element on the page

options.label: D
options.text: Deletes all `<div>` elements from the page

questions.explanationMd:
`document.createElement('div')` creates a new element **in memory**. It is **not** visible on the page until you append it to the DOM:

```js
const div = document.createElement('div');
div.textContent = 'Hello!';
document.body.appendChild(div); // Now it's visible
```

This two-step process lets you configure the element before adding it, which is more efficient than modifying it after insertion.

questions.questionMd: Which method adds a child element **at the end** of a parent's children?
questions.explanationMd:
**`appendChild()`** adds a node as the **last child** of the parent element.

- `insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode)` — inserts before a specific child
- `prepend()` — inserts as the **first** child
- `replaceChild(newChild, oldChild)` — replaces an existing child
- `append()` — similar to `appendChild()` but also accepts strings and multiple arguments

questions.questionMd: How do you add a CSS class to an element without removing existing classes?
questions.explanationMd:
**`classList.add()`** adds one or more classes without affecting existing classes.

```js
element.classList.add('highlight', 'active');
```

Other `classList` methods:
- `.remove('class')` — remove a class
- `.toggle('class')` — add if missing, remove if present
- `.contains('class')` — check if class exists
- `.replace('old', 'new')` — swap a class

⚠️ `className = 'new-class'` and `setAttribute('class', ...)` both **overwrite** all existing classes.

questions.questionMd: What is the advantage of `addEventListener` over using `onclick` property?

options.label: B
options.text: `addEventListener` allows multiple handlers on the same event; `onclick` only allows one

questions.questionMd: In an event handler, what does `event.target` refer to versus `event.currentTarget`?

options.label: A
options.text: They always refer to the same element

options.label: B
options.text: `event.target` is the element that triggered the event; `event.currentTarget` is the element the listener is attached to

options.label: C
options.text: `event.currentTarget` is the element that triggered the event; `event.target` is the listener element

questions.questionMd: What data type does `localStorage.getItem()` always return?

options.label: A
options.text: The original data type that was stored

options.label: B
options.text: A **string** (or `null` if the key doesn't exist)

questions.questionMd: What is the key difference between `localStorage` and `sessionStorage`?

options.label: B
options.text: `localStorage` persists until explicitly cleared; `sessionStorage` is cleared when the browser tab/window is closed

options.label: C
options.text: `sessionStorage` is accessible across all tabs; `localStorage` is not

questions.explanationMd:
**`localStorage`**:
- Persists **indefinitely** (until explicitly cleared by the app or user)
- Shared across all tabs/windows of the same origin
- Typically ~5-10MB

**`sessionStorage`**:
- Cleared when the **tab or window is closed**
- **Not** shared between tabs (each tab gets its own)
- Same storage limit

Both use the same API: `setItem()`, `getItem()`, `removeItem()`, `clear()`

questions.questionMd: The `DOMContentLoaded` event fires after all images and external stylesheets have finished loading.

questions.explanationMd:
Step by step: 1. `items.map(i => \`
  • ${i}
    • \`)` → `['
  • HTTP
    • ', '
  • DOM
    • ', '
  • Events
    • ']` 2. `.join('')` → `'
  • HTTP
  • DOM
  • Events
    • '` (no separator) 3. Template literal wraps it in `
      ...
    ` Without `.join('')`, the array would be implicitly joined with commas (option A). The `.join('')` is the key to clean HTML output. This pattern is commonly used for dynamically building HTML from arrays.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following are valid keyboard event types in the DOM? Select all that apply.
